page.content: In the analysis of health awareness and perceptions, Table 1 presents findings under the header "Statements," detailing responses from participants. The row labeled "Never thought that I needed one" reports 320 responses, constituting 57.2%. The response "Embarrassed to get examined by male health professional" received 134 responses, representing 24%. The sentiment "Afraid to find out cancer" was expressed by 111 individuals, accounting for 19.9%.

responses from participants. The row labeled "Never thought that I needed one" reports 320 responses, constituting 57.2%. The
response "Embarrassed to get examined by male health professional" received 134 responses, representing 24%. The sentiment
"Afraid to find out cancer" was expressed by 111 individuals, accounting for 19.9%.

cells.cell_text: Never thought that I needed one
Embarrassed to get examined by male health professional
Wasn’t aware such services are available in our health facilities
Because of past unpleasant experience with health professionals

page.content: The option "Wasn’t aware such services are available in our health facilities" indicated 34 responses, or 6.1%, while "Because of past unpleasant experience with health professionals" garnered 23 responses, equivalent to 4.1%. Lastly, the "Others" category received 14 responses, making up 2.5%. Table 2 summarizes genotype data with headers "Genotypes," "CAD patients (n = 300)," "Control subjects (n = 312)," and "P-Value." The genotype "C-1065A" shows no data entries. For "CC," there are 154 (51.3%) in CAD patients and 147 (47.1%) in control subjects. The "CA" genotype has 137 (45.7%) among CAD patients and 155 (49.3%) in controls. The "AA" genotype reveals 9 (3.0%) in CAD patients and 10 (3.6%) in controls, with a P-Value of 0.5798#. The "C allele" shows 445 (74.2%) in CAD patients and 449 (72.0%) in controls, while the "A allele" is reported as 155 (25.8%) in CAD patients and 175 (28.0%) in controls, with a P-Value of 0.3834##.
